classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: Writings in Connection with the Donatist Controversy.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
In which Augustin replies to all the several statements in the letter of Petilianus, as though disputing with an adversary face to face.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Petilianus said: "But to us the Lord Christ, in opposition to your deadly commands, commanded simple patience and harmlessness.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: ‘A new commandment I give unto you, That ye love one another; as I have loved you, that ye also love one another.’

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: And again, ‘By this shall all men know that ye are my disciples, if ye have love one to another.’"

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Augustin answered: If you did not transfer these words, so widely differing from your character, to the surface of your talk, how could you be covering yourselves with sheep’s clothing?
